summary:
The calcification of the third molar typically begins around age 8, as indicated by its developmental start within the range of 8 to 15 years. This preempts eruption, which occurs between ages 17 and 22, reflecting a standard pattern where root formation commences long before visible emergence.  

(Note: The summary focuses solely on clinically significant facts related to timing and sequence.)
